collection: Freddy the Foot — Season 1: The 24/7 Club
invariant: one clinical risk -> one habit -> one adventure
delivery_invariant: a little smile that leaves a real reminder
persona_invariant: complains the entire time - does it anyway
thesis_line: I love my recliner. Why? My feet do.
schema_note: v1.1 schema + new fields: peg_beat (off-screen line + Freddy's deadpan), set (primary location). voice_classic unchanged from v1.0.

episodes.title: Freddy Hits the Beach (Under Protest)
episodes.hazard: Thermal burns from hot surfaces (loss of protective sensation)
episodes.habit: Check hot surfaces with your hand before stepping.
episodes.set: beach (recliner cameo: he brought a folding one)

voice_247.wardrobe: Tie-dye, cargo shorts, folding recliner under one arm, cooler in the other
beats: The Sigh: 'Oh good. The beach. Sand in everything for a week. Freddy loves the beach.'
Caption: 'Freddy hasn't felt hot sand since Clinton was in office. The sand does not know this. The sand does not care.'
Freddy, crouched, palm flat on the sand like checking a griddle, muttering. 'His hand still works fine. Unlike his knees. Or his back. Or—' [checks sand anyway]
End card: folding recliner deployed at the waterline, sandals on, drink in hand, at peace against his will.
voice_247.punchline: The sand does not know this. The sand does not care.
voice_247.reminder: Can't feel it doesn't mean it can't burn. Hand check, then walk.
voice_247.video_hook: Freddy was dragged to the beach. He brought his own chair.
voice_247.ugc_prompt: What do you complain about at the beach but secretly love?

episodes.title: Freddy vs. The Dad Shoes
episodes.habit: Break new shoes in slowly and check for hot spots.

voice_247.wardrobe: Garage flannel, staring at a shoebox on the ottoman like it's a summons
beats: The Sigh: 'Oh good. New shoes. Freddy loves change.'
Caption: 'Freddy has opinions about the New Balance. The New Balance does not care. They've reached an understanding.'

Timer on the phone: one hour. Freddy in the recliner, shoes on, arms crossed, watching the clock like it insulted him. Checks for red spots after. 'They're fine. They're... unbelievably comfortable. Tell no one.'
End card: mowing the lawn in them. Fully converted. Will deny it under oath.
voice_247.punchline: The New Balance does not care. They've reached an understanding.
voice_247.reminder: New shoes: one hour at a time. Check for hot spots after every round.

peg_beat.freddy: The doctor doesn't have to LOOK at them, Peg.

voice_247.video_hook: Every guy over 45 knows this exact shoebox.
voice_247.ugc_prompt: Show us the shoes you fought and lost to.

episodes.title: Freddy's Bath: A Trust Exercise
episodes.hazard: Scald burns from bathwater (cannot trust foot sensation)
episodes.habit: Test water with your elbow or a thermometer before getting in.

voice_247.wardrobe: Robe, towel over shoulder, meat thermometer held with complete authority
beats: The Sigh: 'A bath. Sure. Let's negotiate with water again.'
Caption: 'Freddy's feet lie to him about temperature. They've been lying since the 90s. He's stopped asking.'
Meat thermometer in. Pitmaster nod. '98. Perfect brisket. I mean bath.'
End card: soaking, cucumber slices, deeply at peace, would grumble about it if asked.
voice_247.reminder: Feet can't be trusted with temperature. Elbow or thermometer, every time.

peg_beat.freddy: It's OUR meat thermometer, Peg. We're married.

voice_247.video_hook: Why is Freddy holding a meat thermometer in the bathroom?
voice_247.ugc_prompt: Weirdest thing you've used to test bathwater — go.

episodes.title: Freddy's Nightly Address to the Nation
episodes.hazard: Unnoticed sores/cuts on soles (daily inspection gap)
episodes.habit: Check the bottoms of your feet every day with a mirror. Photo it.
episodes.set: THE RECLINER (flagship episode for the set)

voice_247.wardrobe: Robe, reading glasses down, mirror in hand, remote guarded on the armrest, dog eyeing the chair
beats: The Sigh: Freddy settles into the recliner like a king reclaiming a contested throne. 'I love my recliner. Why? My feet do.'
Caption: 'Freddy has seen the bottom of his foot more than his own hairline. He has thoughts about both.'
Mirror check, phone photo, muttering through the whole 40 seconds. 'Freddy did not want to. Freddy did it anyway. This is the whole biography.'
End card: lights low, feet up, dog denied the chair, streak intact.
voice_247.punchline: Freddy did not want to. Freddy did it anyway. This is the whole biography.
voice_247.reminder: 40 seconds a night. Mirror, look, photo. That's the whole job.

peg_beat.peg: I HEARD THE RECLINER. DID YOU CHECK?

voice_247.video_hook: Every night, same chair, same speech. Freddy's nightly address.
voice_247.ugc_prompt: Where does YOUR nightly check happen? Defend your chair.

episodes.title: Freddy at 3AM (The Kitchen Run)
episodes.hazard: Foreign objects / barefoot hazards going unfelt (neuropathy)
episodes.habit: Slides by the bed. Shoes on before walking, even at 3am. Shake them first.

voice_247.wardrobe: Robe, hair-toes askew, phone flashlight, the house is dark and hostile
beats: The Sigh: 3:04 AM. 'Water. At 3AM. Because Freddy's body runs on committee decisions now.'
Caption: 'Freddy found a LEGO in his shoe once at 3am. He never emotionally recovered. The dog has never once apologized.'
Half-asleep, he STILL shakes the slides out before stepping in. Pure autopilot. 'The system works even when Freddy doesn't. Especially then.'
End card: water acquired, dog on HIS recliner in the dark, feet intact. One battle won, one lost.
voice_247.punchline: The dog has never once apologized.
voice_247.reminder: Slides live by the bed. Shake first, then step. Even at 3am.

peg_beat.freddy: [to camera] Thirty years. She can hear bare feet THROUGH A WALL.

voice_247.video_hook: 3AM. The dog has been busy. Freddy has a system.
voice_247.ugc_prompt: What has YOUR floor tried to kill you with at 3am?

episodes.title: Freddy Retires 'It's Nothing'
episodes.hazard: Minor cuts ignored until they become serious wounds
episodes.habit: Clean small cuts immediately. Watch daily. Not healing = call the doc.

voice_247.wardrobe: Grill apron over the tie-dye, tongs, surrounded by guys nodding at things
beats: The Sigh: deck scrape mid-cookout. Every guy in the yard, in unison: 'It's nothing.' The ancient call of their people.
Caption: 'Freddy's dad said "it's nothing" about everything for 60 years. Freddy retired the phrase. The burgers observed a moment of silence.'
Washed, covered, back at the grill in 90 seconds, grumbling the entire time. 'Burgers don't wait. Neither do cuts. Neither does Gary, apparently — GARY, OFF THE GRILL.'
End card: phone reminder set — 'check the nothing' — three days marked. Flame perfect.
voice_247.punchline: The burgers observed a moment of silence.
voice_247.reminder: Small cut: wash it, cover it, watch it daily. Not healing? Make the call.

peg_beat.peg: WAS THAT THE DECK BOARD I ASKED YOU TO FIX?
peg_beat.freddy: ...The cut and I would both like to move on, Peg.

voice_247.video_hook: Three words every guy says. Freddy quit cold turkey.
voice_247.ugc_prompt: What did you once call 'nothing' that taught you a lesson?

episodes.title: Freddy's Toenail Summit
episodes.hazard: Improper nail cutting causing ingrown nails and skin nicks
episodes.habit: Cut straight across, never dig corners. Tough nails = podiatrist.

voice_247.wardrobe: Reading glasses ON, workbench light, clippers laid out with torque-wrench-owner precision
beats: The Sigh: 'Toenail day. The glamour of it all.'
Caption: 'Freddy measures twice and cuts once. His grandfather said that about lumber. Freddy applies it to toenails. Grandpa would have questions.'
Straight across, gentle file, zero corner-digging, running commentary. 'Corners are for lumber. And for the dog when he's in trouble. Not for nails.'
End card: 'The gnarly ones go to Dr. Sole. Freddy knows his trade, and he knows when to call a contractor.'
voice_247.reminder: Straight across. No corner digging. Tough ones go to the pro.

peg_beat.freddy: It's the GARAGE, Peg. Everything in here is the bad rug.

voice_247.video_hook: Freddy treats toenail day like a garage project.
voice_247.ugc_prompt: Rate Freddy's setup. What's on YOUR trimming workbench?

episodes.title: Freddy's Yard: Beautiful, Hostile
episodes.hazard: Puncture wounds from going barefoot outdoors/at home
episodes.habit: Shoes on, always — even at home, even in the yard.

voice_247.wardrobe: Garage flannel, work gloves, sturdy yard shoes, hose, surveying his kingdom with suspicion
beats: The Sigh: 'The lawn looks great. Which means something's wrong.'
Caption: 'Freddy's yard has tried to take him out more times than his 20s did. And his 20s TRIED.'
Yard shoes by every door, slides by the bed, non-negotiable. 'Barefoot is a lifestyle. Freddy's lifestyle is intact skin and low drama.'
End card: golden hour, sprinkler on, folding recliner ON the lawn, shoes ON, king of all he surveys.
voice_247.reminder: Shoes by every door. Barefoot isn't worth the gamble — even on your own grass.

peg_beat.peg: THE DOG BURIED SOMETHING BY THE ROSES.
peg_beat.freddy: Of course he did. It's Tuesday.

voice_247.video_hook: The most dangerous place for your feet? You mow it weekly.
voice_247.ugc_prompt: What's the worst thing hiding in YOUR yard right now? Be honest.

episodes.title: Freddy's Sock Drawer Has Standards
episodes.hazard: Tight elastic and rough seams restricting circulation / causing friction
episodes.habit: Seamless socks, no tight elastic. Change daily. Light colors show problems.

voice_247.wardrobe: Tie-dye, holding an ancient tube sock at arm's length like evidence in a trial
beats: The Sigh: 'Sock audit. Peg's idea. Everything is Peg's idea until it works.'
Caption: 'These socks have been squeezing Freddy since dial-up. They know what they did.'
New standard installed: seamless, soft, loose tops, light colors. 'Freddy's socks now hug. They used to arrest. Growth.'
End card: drawer organized, tube socks demoted to car-wash duty. 'A dignified retirement. More than they deserve.'
voice_247.reminder: Seamless, loose tops, fresh daily. Light colors show trouble early.

peg_beat.freddy: [to camera, wearing the comfortable socks] I don't want to talk about it.

voice_247.video_hook: Freddy's sock drawer audit turned emotional fast.
voice_247.ugc_prompt: How old is the oldest sock you're STILL wearing? Confess.

episodes.title: Freddy & Dr. Sole: 12 Years of Complaints
episodes.habit: Regular podiatrist visits — 'feels fine' still gets checked.

voice_247.wardrobe: The appointment flannel, coffee, judging the waiting-room magazines like it's his job
beats: The Sigh: 'The drive. The parking. The magazines from 2019. Freddy loves appointment day.'
Caption: 'Freddy has complained about coming here for 12 years. He has never once missed it. Dr. Sole stopped listening to the complaints in year two. They're both fine with this.'
Exam, old jokes, real answers. 'Feels fine' gets the full look anyway. 'That's the deal. Freddy doesn't make the rules. Peg does.'
End card: next appointment booked before he's out the door. Fist bump. 'See you in the spring. The magazines better be new.'
voice_247.punchline: Dr. Sole stopped listening to the complaints in year two. They're both fine with this.
voice_247.reminder: 'Feels fine' still gets checked. Keep the standing appointment.

peg_beat.peg: [text on his phone] DID YOU ASK ABOUT THE THING?

voice_247.video_hook: Freddy's longest-running argument? He's never once won it. On purpose.
voice_247.ugc_prompt: Shout out YOUR foot doc. How long have you two been at it?

season_2_backlog: Freddy's Winter Walk (cold injury — grumbles about the boots, wears the boots)
Freddy at the Pool (maceration — 'drying BETWEEN the toes, the forgotten zone')
Freddy's Long Flight (circulation — middle seat, compression socks, opinions)
Freddy Breaks in the Work Boots (job-site footwear)
Freddy's Road Trip (rest-stop walk ritual — Peg navigates, Freddy complies)
Nobody Sits in the Chair (the dog vs. the recliner — bottle episode)

character_canon.freddy: Middle-aged man (early 50s) living with type 2 diabetes. Member of the 24/7 club, not adjacent to it. Every habit is HIS routine, not a demonstration.
character_canon.age_markers: salt-and-pepper brows, graying outer toe-crown, sturdier settled build, reading glasses, laugh lines. Blush retired — reads young.
character_canon.disclosure: Diabetes is canon and matter-of-fact — never the punchline, never explained to the audience, never an inspiration beat.
character_canon.fails_review: Any render/copy where Freddy reads as a kid, teen, young mascot, or outside educator.
